examples: The scrollwork is one of the earliest patterns in the Chinese history, and also is, in the traditional area, one of the most widely, active utilized decorations.
Bas-relief egg-and-dart moldings (with alternating oval and pointed forms), and scrollwork such as that found on Ionic capitals and in the running-dog pattern (or wave scroll).
The shape is entirely within the bounds of late baroque taste, and no important asymmetrical motifs are evident in the elaborate scrollwork except in a decorative cartouche on the top.
